content.claim: School closures' effects on child academic outcomes are contested internationally. Negative-effect studies report test-score declines of 5.9% of a standard deviation and high-school dropout increases of 49–68 percentage points in Chile; Danish and Amsterdam studies find no negative impacts or effects that fade over time.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Evidence is split by country context (Denmark, Chile, Amsterdam) and closure type (accountability-driven vs. enrollment-driven)
No Hungarian-specific randomized or quasi-experimental evidence on academic outcomes of rural school closures
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
